summary: A comprehensive test manuscript record containing all available fields. Two-part palimpsest codex: Part 1 contains 10th-century Arabic Gospels (Naskh) over a 5th-century Greek undertext; Part 2 is a 13th-century Arabic Lectionary supplement.

part.label: Part 1
part.summary: Main text block, palimpsested parchment with later paper replacements

translation: Index of the four Gospels
note: Introductory index added to the part later; does not belong to the main overtext layer

note.value: Ff. 1-10 are paper replacements; remaining folios are parchment
Quire signatures partially visible in lower margin, recto side

related_mss.label: Closely related overtext layers
note: Overtext of Part 1 shares script tradition with Sinai Arabic 72 and Sinai Arabic 116
Clear Naskh hand throughout
Section headings in a larger, more formal hand
Kufic used for chapter headings in the opening quire only
Main text in brown ink
Chapter numbers in black
Rubrication in red throughout first quires
Gold used for decorated initials
Ruled in drypoint
Text written below top line
Layout shifts to two columns in final quires

text_unit_record.label: Arabic Gospels
text_unit_record.summary: The four Gospels in Arabic translation

translation: The Gospel of Matthew the Apostle

excerpt.as_written: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم كتاب ميلاد يسوع المسيح
translation: In the name of God the compassionate, the merciful. The book of the birth of Jesus Christ
note: Standard Arabic Christian opening formula precedes the incipit

excerpt.as_written: وها أنا معكم كل الأيام إلى انقضاء الدهر
translation: And lo, I am with you always, even to the end of the age

contents.label: Matthew 1–7
note: Sermon on the Mount begins f. 5r
Text closely related to Kashouh family 1

bib.citation: Kashouh, Hikmat. The Arabic Versions of the Gospels. Berlin: De Gruyter, 2012.

translation: ...and Jesus Christ, the Son of God
note: Text begins imperfectly; opening lines missing

translation: And they went out and preached everywhere...
note: Text ends imperfectly; remainder lost
Text begins imperfectly; translator tradition uncertain

bib.citation: Kashouh, Hikmat. The Arabic Versions of the Gospels. Berlin: De Gruyter, 2012.

excerpt.as_written: إذ كان كثيرون قد أخذوا بتأليف قصة
translation: Since many have undertaken to compile a narrative
The Gospel of Luke the Physician is completed

note: Translation closely related to Sinai Arabic 72

bib.citation: Kashouh, Hikmat. The Arabic Versions of the Gospels. Berlin: De Gruyter, 2012.

para.as_written: كتب هذا الإنجيل المبارك في شهر رمضان المعظم على يد الخاطئ يوحنا بن عيسى الكاتب
translation: This blessed Gospel was written in the blessed month of Ramadan by the hand of the sinner Yuhanna ibn Isa al-Katib
